reasoning: The informal statement defines $g(x) = \lim_{r \to 0}((x+1)^{r+1} - x^{r+1})^{1/r}$ (two-sided) and asks for $\lim_{x \to \infty} g(x)/x$. The Lean formulation uses `𝓝[>] 0` (right-sided limit) in the hypothesis defining $g$. For this specific expression, expanding $(x+1)^{r+1} - x^{r+1} = 1 + rL + O(r^2)$ with $L = (x+1)\ln(x+1) - x\ln x$, both one-sided limits (and hence the two-sided limit) exist and equal $e^L$. So any $g$ satisfying the right-sided limit condition has the same values on $(0,\infty)$ as any $g$ satisfying the two-sided limit condition. The quantifier `∀ x > 0` matches "for every positive real number $x$", and the conclusion `Tendsto (g x / x) atTop (𝓝 (Real.exp 1))` matches the informal limit and answer $e$. The two theorems are therefore logically equivalent and faithful.
